title: entrypoint arg for buildah generated images are not same as docker.
body:
```
#!/bin/sh

container=$(buildah from docker://debian:latest)
mount="$(buildah mount $container)"

cmd_sh='#!/bin/sh\n
echo "This is cmd with arg $@"'

entry_sh='#!/bin/sh\n
echo "This is cmd with arg $@"'

echo -e $cmd_sh > $mount/bin/cmd.sh
echo -e $entry_sh > $mount/bin/entry.sh

chmod a+x $mount/bin/{cmd,entry}.sh

buildah config --cmd "cmd.sh" $container
buildah config --entrypoint "entry.sh" $container

buildah commit --rm --format docker $container docker-test

# buildah rm $container
# buildah umount $container

archive=$(tempfile)
buildah push docker-test docker-archive:$archive
loaded_id=$(cat $archive | docker load | grep Loaded |sed -e 's/Loaded image ID: //')

docker tag $loaded_id buildah-bug

echo -e "FROM $loaded_id\nCMD [\"cmd.sh\"]\nENTRYPOINT [\"entry.sh\"]" | docker build . -f - -t buildah-nobug

if [ ! -z "$loaded_id" ]; then
    rm -f $archive
fi

# Script end
```
Running the above script, and do something like
docker run buildah-bug
docker run buildah-nobug

Thanks!

comments.body:
I'm not sure exactly what you're example is trying to demonstrate, what is the expected output / actual output?

There are two forms of ```--entrypoint```, I suspect this may be why you aren't seeing the behaviour you expect.

The documentation here describes the different behaviours of values supplied to ```--entrypoint```
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/docs/buildah-config.md
Thanks, Please close this, It works as expected!
